_comment: Spec for the 'Follow the PAC' Helm mailer feed. Apply via the Helm MCP `create_feed` tool against the PAC project, or create manually in the Helm Dashboard under Marketing > Feeds.

feed.name: Follow the PAC
feed.description: Monthly note ahead of each PAC gathering — date, time, what to bring, and the occasional behind-the-scenes from the families building it. Right after the 9:30 Mass at Immaculate Conception, East Aurora.

signup_form.consent_text: One click to unsubscribe whenever. We'll never share your address.
signup_form.success_message:
★ YOU'RE ON THE LIST.

See you in the gym after the 9:30. Watch for the next note.

(In the meantime — tell another family.)

welcome_email._note: First touch after signup. Send via send_email or schedule for ~5 minutes after subscribe.
welcome_email.subject: You're on the list — Follow the PAC
welcome_email.body_html:
Follow the PAC

Hey {first_name} — glad you're in.

Here's what ‘Follow the PAC’ is: about one short email a month, ahead of each gathering. Date, time, what to bring, and the occasional note from the families building this thing.

Next PAC: Sunday, May 3 — right after the 9:30 Mass, in the gym.
What to bring: the kids and whatever joy you have left after getting them to Mass.

That's it for now. See you Sunday.

— The PAC

Immaculate Conception, East Aurora, NY. Manage preferences.

monthly_template._note: Template to schedule ~7 days before each PAC. Fill in date and what-to-bring.
monthly_template.subject: Sunday: PAC — {first_name}, the kids, and the gym
monthly_template.body_html_skeleton:
PAC — [MONTH DAY]

Hey {first_name} —

This Sunday. Right after the 9:30 Mass, in the gym.

What to bring: [WHAT TO BRING].

See you in there.

— The PAC

voice_check: Subject lines: short, concrete, never 'Don't miss out!' or '✨ Exciting news!'
No 'Dear friend in Christ' / 'Beloved parishioner' / 'Faith family' openers
Logistics block always includes 'after the 9:30 Mass' and 'in the gym'
End every monthly with a tiny contribution opener — 'tell another family', 'bring a snack', 'come help set up'
